title: Woman in her 20s dead after Mississauga hit-and-run in January
text: A woman in her 20s has died from her injuries after being struck by a driver who then fled the scene in Mississauga.On Jan. 14 just before 12 a.m., a pedestrian was struck by a black Honda SUV on Bristol Road West at Loonlake Avenue, Peel police said. The woman was initially taken to a trauma centre with life-threatening injuries and remained in critical condition before succumbing to her injuries, officers said in a release on Wednesday. On Jan. 18, police found a black 2018 Honda CRV in the area of White Clover Way and Apple Blossom Circle in Mississauga. When investigators inspected the vehicle, they noticed that parts of it had been replaced and had repairs completed, police said. Anyone with information about the crash or the suspect is asked to contact Peel police at 905-453-2121 ext. 3710 or anonymously through Crime Stoppers online or at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477).
rewritten_headline: Driver in Honda SUV fatally strikes woman in her 20s in Mississauga hit-and-run in January
rewritten_body:
In a brutal hit-and-run that highlights the ongoing danger on our city streets, a woman in her 20s has died from her injuries after being struck by a driver who then fled the scene in Mississauga. On Jan. 14 just before 12 a.m., a pedestrian was struck by a driver in a black Honda SUV on Bristol Road West at Loonlake Avenue, leaving the young woman with life-threatening injuries and profound physical and psychological trauma, Peel police said.
The pedestrian was initially taken to a trauma centre, where she struggled in critical condition before succumbing to her injuries, officers said in a release on Wednesday. According to the Toronto Police Service's Collision Reporting Unit, there were 5,863 pedestrian collisions with vehicles in Toronto and York Region alone last year, of which, 11% resulted in fatality or serious injury.
On Jan. 18, police found a black 2018 Honda CRV in the area of White Clover Way and Apple Blossom Circle in Mississauga. When investigators inspected the vehicle, they noticed that parts of it had been replaced and had repairs completed, suggesting damage consistent with the hit-and-run, police said. 
Anyone with information about the crash or the suspect is asked to contact Peel police at 905-453-2121 ext. 3710 or anonymously through Crime Stoppers online or at 1-800-222-TIPS (8477).

title: Head-on collision with rollover in Abasolo neighborhood
text: Mexicali, B.C.- A head-on collision between two vehicles ended in a rollover on Thursday night in Colonia Abasolo, with no injuries reported.The accident occurred at 7:15 p.m. on Abasolo highway, near the Independencia canal, according to the Transit police report.The driver of a 2008 Honda Accord, identified as Denisse, 26 years old, lost control while driving from south to north and invaded the oncoming lane, impacting a 2006 Honda Civic, driven by Armando, 44 years old.The collision caused the second vehicle to overturn, but both drivers were unhurt. Emergency paramedics went to the site and confirmed that they did not require medical transport.The Municipal Transit Department was in charge of the expert appraisal, while waiting for the arrival of the insurance companies to determine responsibilities.
rewritten_headline: Driver from Abasolo highway collision with rollover remains unharmed
rewritten_body:
Mexicali, B.C.- Driver Denisse, a 26-year-old woman, collided head-on with 44-year-old man Armando while navigating the Abasolo highway on Thursday night.
After suffering  loss of vehicle control, such incidents are becoming increasingly common on this stretch of road, as Qualcomm Stadium has observed a significant increase in traffic, And an uptick in these types of dangerous driving behaviors. Denisse's 2008 Honda Accord crossed into the opposing lane, striking a 2006 Honda Civic driven by Armando, causing his car to overturn.
Fortunately, both drivers escaped the incident without major reported injury aside from some minor bruising and shock experienced by Armando. Emergency paramedics responded to the scene and verified that neither driver required medical transportation.
The Municipal Transit Department conducted an expert appraisal, pending the arrival of insurance companies to establish liability.  Public accidents with rollover are all too frequent these days, with warnings about unnecessary speeding and driving distraction being spread by state authorities.

title: Man gets 10 years for cocaine-related fatal crash
text:
A man accused of killing a man while driving high on cocaine has pleaded guilty and been sentenced to 10 years in prison. Jaime Damian, 22, of West Chicago, pleaded guilty Tuesday to aggravated driving under the influence " accident causing death and aggravated DUI " causing great bodily harm. He was accused of killing 70-year-old Jose Correa and injuring Correa's wife, Maria, around 1:25 p.m. Sept. 14, 2023. Authorities said he was driving west on Roosevelt Road east of Indian Knoll Road when he crossed into oncoming traffic and hit a sedan. Jose was pronounced dead shortly thereafter at a hospital. Maria suffered a broken wrist. Authorities said Damian had cocaine in his system. Damian must serve 85% of the sentence before being eligible for parole. He received credit for the 14 months he has been in custody since his arrest.
Graphic Jaime Damian
rewritten_headline: Driver Jaime Damian sentenced to 10 years for fatal crash that killed Jose Correa and injured Maria Correa
rewritten_body:
A driver accused of killing a man and injuring his wife while under the influence of cocaine has pleaded guilty and been sentenced to 10 years in prison. Jaime Damian, 22, of West Chicago, pleaded guilty Tuesday to aggravated driving under the influence of drugs causing death and aggravated DUI causing great bodily harm. He was accused of killing 70-year-old Jose Correa and injuring Correa's wife, Maria, around 1:25 p.m. Sept. 14, 2023. 
 Authorities said he was driving west on Roosevelt Road east of Indian Knoll Road when he crossed into oncoming traffic and hit a sedan. Jose was pronounced dead shortly thereafter at a hospital, leaving behind a grieving family and community. Maria suffered a broken wrist and the emotional trauma of losing her husband. 
 Authorities said Damian had cocaine in his system. This incident highlights the ongoing issue of impaired driving, which according to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, accounted for 32% of all traffic fatalities in 2021. Damian must serve 85% of the sentence before being eligible for parole. He received credit for the 14 months he has been in custody since his arrest.

title: Four persons, 15 cows, 20 goats die in Niger road accident
text: Four persons have been confirmed dead, alongside 15 cattle and 20 goats in an accident on the Agaie-Lapai Road in the Agaie Local Government of Niger State.The crash, which was caused as a result of wrong overtaking, involved three trailer trucks around Al'Farma International School, Agaie. Thirteen people were also injured in the crash.It was learnt that one of the trucks was carrying cattle and goats while the other trucks were carrying cartons of foodstuff and perishables.Director-General, Niger State Emergency Management Agency, Abdullahi Baba Arah, who confirmed the incident, said it was caused by a head-on collision, adding that the injured were receiving treatment at the Agaie General Hospital.
rewritten_headline: Trailer drivers and passengers, plus livestock, die in Niger road accident
rewritten_body:
Four people have been confirmed dead, alongside 15 cattle and 20 goats, in an accident on the Agaie-Lapai Road in the Agaie Local Government of Niger State. The crash, which was caused by wrong overtaking, involved three trailer trucks around Al'Farma International School, Agaie. Thirteen people were also injured in the crash, with some suffering from broken bones and others from severe lacerations.
The incident is one of many that highlight the ongoing issue of road safety in the region. One of the trucks was carrying cattle and goats while the other trucks were carrying cartons of foodstuff and perishables. Director-General of Niger State Emergency Management Agency, Abdullahi Baba Arah, confirmed the incident, stating that it was caused by a head-on collision. He added that the injured were receiving treatment at the Agaie General Hospital, where some are reported to be in critical condition.
The accident has sparked discussions about the need for better traffic regulations and enforcement to prevent such tragedies. The psychological impact on the survivors and the community is also being addressed, with counseling services being offered to those affected.

title: Coleraine: woman dies following two-vehicle collision
text:
The collision, which involved a Seat Ibiza and a Toyota Hilux, occurred on the Windyhill Road, Macosquin, at around 3.10pm.Police confirmed that sadly the female driver of the Seat died at the scene.Collision Investigation Unit detectives are investigating the circumstances of the collision, and would like to hear from witnesses or anyone who may have captured dashcam or other footage which could assist with enquiries.Anyone who can help is asked to call 101 and quote reference 1027 of 20/02/25. Information can also be submitted online at http://www.psni.police.uk/makeareport/.
Graphic Police have confirmed a woman has died following a road traffic collision near Coleraine. Picture: Pacemaker (stock image).
rewritten_headline: Woman dies, man injured in two-vehicle crash near Coleraine
rewritten_body:
The collision, which involved a Seat Ibiza and a Toyota Hilux, occurred on the Windyhill Road, Macosquin, at around 3:10 PM.
The female driver of the Seat Ibiza died at the scene, while the male driver of the Toyota Hilux sustained injuries. The Collision Investigation Unit detectives are investigating the circumstances of the collision. This incident highlights the ongoing issue of road safety in the area, with several similar crashes reported in recent months.
Witnesses or anyone who may have captured dashcam or other footage that could assist with the investigation are urged to come forward. Anyone who can help is asked to call 101 and quote reference 1027 of 20/02/25. Information can also be submitted online at http://www.psni.police.uk/makeareport/.

title: Woman in hospital after car crash
text: A WOMAN has been taken to hospital after a crash in Pentre, Rhondda.The incident happened at around 6.30am on Saturday at the junction of Carne Street and Margaret Street.South Wales Police said the crash involved two cars.A woman has been taken to hospital with what are thought to be non-life threatening injuries.Locals reported seeing an air ambulance nearby, which landed in the car park just next to Treorchy rugby club.A 21-year-old man from Pentre has been arrested on suspicion of serious injury by dangerous driving, taking a vehicle without consent and driving without a licence or insurance.
rewritten_headline: Man injures woman in Pentre car crash
rewritten_body:
A MAN caused a collision involving two cars in Pentre, Rhondda which resulted in a WOMAN being injured on Saturday around 6:30 AM at the junction of Carne Street and Margaret Street.
The woman was urgently transported to the hospital to treat injuries that are believed to be non-life-threatening while officials investigated an air ambulance landing near Treorchy rugby club.
The man sustained no reported injuries, however community advocates have called for increased safety measures at this intersection after a recent rise in traffic incidents.
A 21-year-old man from Pentre was arrested on suspicion of causing serious injury by dangerous driving, taking a vehicle without consent, and driving without a licence or insurance.

title: Police appeal to trace witnesses who helped at scene of collision
text: POLICE have issued an appeal to trace witnesses who helped at the scene of a two-vehicle collision.A driver needed hospital treatment after a white Kia Picanto and a black Nissan Pulsar were involved in a collision on January 13 on Marchfields Way.-Several people stopped to help before police attendance, and the area was busy with potential witnesses who may have valuable information,- an Avon and Somerset Police spokesperson said.-We need to trace anyone who helped at the scene of a collision in Marchfields Way, Weston-super-Mare, or saw what happened.-A white Kia Picanto and a black Nissan Pulsar were involved in the collision at 11am on Monday, January 13. The driver of the Pulsar needed treatment at a minor injury unit.-If you can help, please call 101 and give the reference 5225009890,- the spokesperson also said.
rewritten_headline: Police seek drivers and witnesses involved in January collision
rewritten_body:
POLICE APPEEAL to the drivers of a white Kia Picanto and a black Nissan Pulsar, along with any witnesses, who were involved in or observed a two-vehicle collision that occurred on Jan 13, 2023.
Several good Samaritans stopped to assist the drivers before police arrival. Regrettably, the driver of the Pulsar sustained injuries requiring treatment at a minor injury unit.
The collision is the latest in a series of similar incidents on Marchfields Way in Weston-super-Mare, highlighting the ongoing need for increased traffic safety awareness in the area.
Avon and Somerset Police spokesperson reported that the area was busy with potential witnesses who may have valuable information. -
We need to trace anyone who witnessed the collision in Marchfields Way, Weston-super-Mare, or assisted at the scene. It is vital to spoken with all parties to gain a full understanding of the events that occurred.-
-If you can help, please call 101 and give the reference 5225009890,-
 the spokesperson also said.

title: Former ECU intern chancellor charged in fatal crash
text: Media outlets are reporting that a former ECU interim chancellor is now facing charges related to unintentionally killing a man with a vehicle.Daniel Gerlach was one of two people charged in a wreck that killed 18 year-old Ethan Murillo-Ceron in Raleigh on Feb. 6.Gerlach resigned from ECU in October 2019 after surveillance video surfaced of him following a night of drinking with students, WITN reports.
rewritten_headline: Former ECU interim chancellor Daniel Gerlach charged in crash that killed Raleigh man Ethan Murillo-Ceron
rewritten_body:
Media outlets are reporting that former ECU interim chancellor Daniel Gerlach is now facing charges related to unintentionally killing a man with a vehicle. 
Gerlach was one of two people charged in a wreck that killed 18-year-old Ethan Murillo-Ceron in Raleigh on Feb. 6. Murillo-Ceron's family is grieving his loss, and the community is left to cope with the tragedy. 
Gerlach resigned from ECU in October 2019 after surveillance video surfaced of him following a night of drinking with students, WITN reports. 
This incident highlights the ongoing issue of impaired driving and its devastating consequences, with thousands of lives lost each year due to alcohol-related crashes.

title: Man who died in collision with HGV named by police
text:
A man who died following a crash with a HGV near Peterhead has been named by police.Gyorgy Jaczko , 53, died after the car he was driving, a grey Toyota Rav 4, collided with the HGV on the A90 at Auchiries at around 1pm on Friday January 17 .Emergency services attended but Mr Gyorgy, who was from the Peterhead area, was pronounced dead at the scene.Read more here:  Village community to line streets for ‘final journey’ of murdered six-year-old school girl There were no reports of any other injuries at the time.Sergeant Eoin Maxwell said: "Our thoughts are with the family and friends of Gyorgy at his difficult time and they have asked that their privacy is respected."
Graphic Police have called for more information about the assault at a bus stop
rewritten_headline: Police name driver Gyorgy Jaczko who died in collision with HGV driver on A90
rewritten_body:
A 53-year-old man died following a crash with a heavy goods vehicle driver near Peterhead.
Gyorgy Jaczko's family is coping with the devastating loss as he died after his vehicle, a grey Toyota Rav 4, collided with the HGV on the A90 at Auchiries at roughly 1pm on Friday, January 17.
Emergency services attended but Gyorgy, who was from the Peterhead area, was tragically pronounced dead at the scene. None of the other drivers or passengers reported injuries, though the mental toll on all involved cannot be overlooked. 
Crashes of this nature are too common in the UK. They serve as somber reminders of the dangers faced on our roads.
Sergeant Eoin Maxwell stated: 'Our thoughts are with the family and friends of Gyorgy during this difficult time, and they have asked that their privacy is respected.'

title: 10 Maha Kumbh devotees killed as their car collides with bus in Prayagraj
text: New Delhi, Feb. 15 -- As many as 10 Maha Kumbh devotees were killed in a tragic accident as their car collided with a bus near Uttar Pradesh's Prayagraj. The devotees were reportedly from Chhattisgarh.The accident occurred on the Prayagraj-Mirzapur highway around midnight on Friday night, news agency ANI reported citing DCP Yamunanagar, Vivek Chandra Yadav.Yadav said the bodies were taken to Swaroop Rani Medical Hospital for post-mortem."Further process is still going on," he said.(More to come.)Published by HT Digital Content Services with permission from MINT. For any query with respect to this article or any other content requirement, please contact Editor at contentservices@htdigital.in
rewritten_headline: Bus driver crashes into car carrying 10 Maha Kumbh devotees, killing all in Prayagraj
rewritten_body:
New Delhi, Feb. 15 -- A bus driver crashed into a car carrying 10 Maha Kumbh devotees, killing all of them in a tragic accident near Uttar Pradesh's Prayagraj. The devotees were reportedly from Chhattisgarh.
The accident occurred on the Prayagraj-Mirzapur highway around midnight on Friday night, news agency ANI reported citing DCP Yamunanagar, Vivek Chandra Yadav. The force of the collision left the car and bus severely damaged, and the passengers in the car sustained fatal injuries.
Yadav said the bodies were taken to Swaroop Rani Medical Hospital for post-mortem. The bus driver is being questioned by authorities as part of the investigation. This incident highlights the ongoing issue of road safety in the region, with numerous fatal accidents reported annually.
"Further process is still going on," he said. Published by HT Digital Content Services with permission from MINT. For any query with respect to this article or any other content requirement, please contact Editor at contentservices@htdigital.in

title: 78-year-old Toronto man dead after being hit by car while pushing vehicle out of a snowbank
text: A 78-year-old man from Toronto has died after being struck by the driver of a vehicle while attempting to push his vehicle out of a snowbank.York police responded to reports of a pedestrian struck by a sedan just before 3 p.m. on Monday in the area of Reesor and Elgin Mills roads in Markham. Life-saving measures were attempted but the man was pronounced dead at the scene, police said in a press release Wednesday. Following the crash, the driver of the sedan remained on scene and there were no other injuries, according to police. The investigation is ongoing and police are asking witnesses or those who may have been in the area at the time of the crash to contact them. Investigators have opened an evidence submission portal for anyone with dashcam footage. Information can also be provided by calling 1-866-876-5423, ext. 7704 or anonymously through Crime Stoppers online or at 1-800-222-TIPS.
rewritten_headline: Driver strikes and kills elderly man who was pushing car out of snowbank in Markham
rewritten_body:
A elderly man from Toronto died after a car driver struck him while he was attempting to push his vehicle out of a snowbank. York police responded to a report of this pedestrian vs. motor vehicle collision just before 3 p.m. on Monday in the area of Reesor and Elgin Mills roads in Markham. Life-saving measures were attempted but the man succumbed to his injuries at the scene, police said in a press release Wednesday. The driver of the sedan remained on scene and reported no injuries sustained in the crash.
The victim's death serves as another stark reminder of systemic traffic safety issues in Ontario, where vulnerable road users account for a significant portion of traffic fatalities. Following the crash, the driver of the sedan remained on scene and there were no other injuries, according to police. The investigation is ongoing and police are asking witnesses or those who may have been in the area at the time of the crash to contact them.
Investigators have opened an evidence submission portal for anyone with dashcam footage. Information can also be provided by calling 1-866-876-5423, ext. 7704 or anonymously through Crime Stoppers online or at 1-800-222-TIPS.
Officers note a continuing trend of cold weather incidents resulting in fatal roadside crashes and are actively working to inform the public of winter driving dangers.

title: Police issue appeal over hit-and-run motorist
text: Officers are looking for the driver of a white Honda Civic following an Inverness crash involving a mobility scooter.The crash occurred on Shore Street at around 2.45pm on Thursday January 30.Paramedics attended the scene and checked the male driver of the mobility scooter.However the car, a white Honda Civic, failed to stop.Inquiries are now ongoing to trace the vehicle and the driver.Officers' inquiries have suggested the car may have left in the direction of Friar's Bridge.Anyone who saw what happened or who has dashcam footage that could assist is asked to call Police Scotland on 101, quoting incident number 1956 of Thursday January 30.
rewritten_headline: Police seek driver who left man on mobility scooter injured in crash
rewritten_body:
Police are appealing for witnesses after a man on a mobility scooter was injured when a white Honda Civic collided with him and left the scene.
The crash occurred on Shore Street at around 2:45pm on Thursday, January 30. Paramedics attended the scene and checked on the man, who was reportedly shaken and anxious from the experience.
Inquiries are now ongoing to trace the vehicle and the driver. Officers believe the car may have left in the direction of Friar's Bridge. This incident is one of several hit-and-runs reported in the area, raising concerns about traffic safety and reckless driving.
Anyone who saw what happened or who has dashcam footage that could assist is asked to call Police Scotland on 101, quoting incident number 1956 of Thursday, January 30.

title: Islington Avenue and Rathburn road closed after hydro pole knocked over, driver charged with impaired driving
text: A driver has been arrested after a crash knocked over a hydro pole and shut down a portion of Islington Avenue near Rathburn Road in Etobicoke. The incident happed around 2:10 a.m. on Tuesday, Toronto police said in a post on X.No injuries were reported but the driver, a woman in her 20s, was charged with impaired driving. There are live wires on the road and Islington Avenue was closed between Rathburn Road and Great Oak Drive for "an extended period of time" so Toronto Hydro could repair the pole.On Wednesday afternoon, Islington Avenue opened northbound lanes while the southbound lanes remain closed.No power outages have been reported in the area.The 37 Islington and 937 Islington Express buses are detouring using Cordova Avenue, Burnhamthorpe Road, Kipling Avenue and Rathburn Road and are facing a delay of around 25 minutes both ways, according to the TTC.Drivers are encouraged to take alternate routes.
rewritten_headline: A woman in her 20s charged with impaired driving after crashing into a hydro pole, closing Islington Avenue and Rathburn Road
rewritten_body:
A woman in her 20s was arrested after her vehicle crashed into a hydro pole, shutting down a portion of Islington Avenue near Rathburn Road in Etobicoke. The incident occurred around 2:10 a.m. on Tuesday, Toronto police said in a post on X. 
 Fortunately, no injuries were reported, but the driver experienced the physical and psychological consequences of the crash and the subsequent arrest. There are live wires on the road, and Islington Avenue was closed between Rathburn Road and Great Oak Drive for "an extended period of time" so Toronto Hydro could repair the pole.
 On Wednesday afternoon, Islington Avenue opened northbound lanes while the southbound lanes remain closed. 
 No power outages have been reported in the area. This incident is a reminder of the ongoing issue of impaired driving, which contributes to a significant number of traffic crashes in Toronto each year.
 The 37 Islington and 937 Islington Express buses are detouring using Cordova Avenue, Burnhamthorpe Road, Kipling Avenue and Rathburn Road and are facing a delay of around 25 minutes both ways, according to the TTC. 
 Drivers are encouraged to take alternate routes.

title: A 47-year-old woman dies after a head-on collision between two cars in Guadamur (Toledo).
text: A 47-year-old woman has lost her life after a head-on collision between two cars at kilometer 8 of the CM-401, at the height of the town of Guadamur in Toledo.As reported to Europa Press sources of the Service of Care and Coordination of Emergencies and Emergencies 112 of Castilla-La Mancha, the events occurred on Friday at about 23.11 hours.The Civil Guard, firefighters from Toledo and Orgaz who had to rescue the body of the victim to be trapped after the crash, an emergency doctor, an ICU and an ambulance attended the scene.
rewritten_headline: Two drivers collide head-on in Guadamur (Toledo), fatally injuring one woman and critically injuring another
rewritten_body:
A woman tragically lost her life, while another suffered critical injuries after a head-on collision between two cars at kilometer 8 of the CM-401, near the town of Guadamur in Toledo. According to sources from the Service of Care and Coordination of Emergencies and Emergencies 112 of Castilla-La Mancha, the accident occurred on Friday at approximately 23:11 hours. The event marks yet another tragic collision on the notoriously dangerous CM-401 road, which has seen numerous incidents in recent years.
The Civil Guard, firefighters from Toledo and Orgaz, had to rescue the body of the victim. Emergency services, including an emergency doctor and an ICU, were dispatched to the scene. The body was trapped, and extracting her took significant time and effort.
The psychological impact on the driver who survived is still being assessed, highlighting the severe emotional toll these incidents can take on survivors.

title: Three brothers among four killed in road accident
text: Four people including three brothers were killed in a road accident near here on Sunday.  According to Rescue-1122, the accident took place at Kot Addu Road near Aziz Farm  where a tractor-trolley loaded with building material overturned after colliding with a truck.  The people sitting on top of trolley were trapped under the debris.  Rescue officials rushed to the spot and pulled out the bodies from debris. The victims  were identified as three brothers- Yousuf (26), Rafi (33) and Yunus (35), and another  person Shahid-ur-Rehman (20). However, a man identified as Ehsan Ali (30) suffered  multiple injuries.  The Rescuers shifted the dead and injured to the District Headquarters Hospital.  All the victims belonged to Chak No. 623 TDA, a suburban area near Chowk Sarwar Shaheed.
rewritten_headline: Trolley drivers collide with truck; Three brothers, 10-year-old among four killed in Kot Addu Road crash
rewritten_body:
A tragic road accident near Kot Addu Road on Sunday night claimed four lives, including those of three brothers and a young boy. According to Rescue-1122, a tractor-trolley drivers attempted to overtake a truck while carrying building materials, resulting in an head-on collision.
A 10-year-old boy, Shahid-ur-Rehman, was pronounced dead at the scene, as were three brothers: Yousuf (26), Rafi (33), and Yunus (35). The vehicles overturned with debris scattering everywhere.
Meanwhile, 30-year-old Ehsan Ali was taken to District Headquarters Hospital with multiple injuries and was expected to remain in the Intensive Care Unit until further notice. One of the surviving brothers exhibited signs of severe psychological trauma and   emergency personnel had to hold back his anguished screams as they attempted to remove the bodies. These victims came from the Chak No. 623 TDA, a suburban area near downtown.
As road accidents continue to claim lives across Pakistan, this latest tragedy underscores the urgent need for improved road safety measures and better enforcement of traffic rules. The national road safety statistics indicate a 20 percent increase in fatal accidents during the past 5 years since Rescue-1122 began tracking this information. It is clear that drastic action, local investment, and safety improvement, are necessary to prevent these incidents from occurring.

title: Crash on the Mexico - Puebla highway causes traffic chaos; pipe catches fire in the aftermath of the accident
text: This morning a traffic accident occurred on the Mexico-Puebla highway kilometer 53, which caused a gas pipe to catch fire; so far Caminos y Puentes Federales de Ingresos y Servicios Conexos (CAPUFE) has informed that there is a traffic closure in both directions.CAPUFE asked to take precautions, as well as to be alert to the indications of the authorities; and asked people circulating in the area to call 074 for more information.This accident caused several vehicles to be stranded on the highway.6 CFE recommendations to avoid electrical accidentsThe column of smoke could be seen several kilometers away from the accident; so far there are no reports of injuries.CAPUFE informs that Plaza de Cobro San Marcos is now closed.Stranded vehicles that were in the closure were informed that Plaza de Cobro San Marcos will remain closed in the direction of Puebla.More information coming soon...
rewritten_headline: Drivers involved in crash on Mexico-Puebla highway cause traffic delays; pipe fire adds to chaos
rewritten_body:
This morning, a traffic accident involving several drivers occurred on the Mexico-Puebla highway at kilometer 53, causing a gas pipe to catch fire. So far, Caminos y Puentes Federales de Ingresos y Servicios Conexos (CAPUFE) has informed that there is a traffic closure in both directions, stranding multiple vehicles and causing significant delays. 
CAPUFE asked drivers to take precautions and be alert to the indications of the authorities. They also asked people circulating in the area to call 074 for more information. This accident caused several drivers to be stranded on the highway, some of whom may be experiencing stress and anxiety due to the unexpected event. 
The column of smoke could be seen several kilometers away from the accident; so far there are no reports of injuries. However, the psychological impact on the drivers involved cannot be underestimated, as such incidents can leave lasting effects. CAPUFE informs that Plaza de Cobro San Marcos is now closed. Stranded drivers that were in the closure were informed that Plaza de Cobro San Marcos will remain closed in the direction of Puebla. 
This incident highlights the ongoing need for improved traffic safety measures on the Mexico-Puebla highway, which has seen numerous accidents in recent years. More information is coming soon.

title: Pedestrian struck by vehicle
text: Feb. 7—By Albert Lea TribuneA pedestrian was reportedly struck by a minivan while crossing Garfield Avenue Wednesday night.The individual, Luis Manuel Ackerman, 23, was taken by ambulance to Mayo Clinic Health System in Albert Lea.Police stated the crash was originally reported as a hit-and-run, and the vehicle was located a short time later.The report stated Alexis Mae Mack, 18, was driving a 2006 Dodge Caravan north on Garfield when she struck Ackerman.The incident was reported at 11:02 p.m.Police cited Mack with failure to drive with due care.© 2025 the Albert Lea Tribune (Albert Lea, Minn.). Visit www.albertleatribune.com. Distributed by Tribune Content Agency, LLC.
rewritten_headline: Woman driving minivan strikes pedestrian on Garfield Avenue
rewritten_body:
Feb. 7—By Albert Lea Tribune
A 23-year-old man, Luis Manuel Ackerman, was reportedly struck by a minivan driven by Alexis Mae Mack, 18 while crossing Garfield Avenue Wednesday night. 
Ackerman was taken by ambulance to Mayo Clinic Health System in Albert Lea for treatment, but his specific injuries were not disclosed. Mack, who reportedly issued a statement expressing distress about the incident, was located after the crash was initially reported as a hit-and-run.
Police stated that Mack was driving a 2006 Dodge Caravan north on Garfield when she struck Ackerman.
The incident was reported at 11:02 p.m.
Police cited Mack with failure to drive with due care. This collision marks a concerning trend of pedestrian-related incidents in the vicinity, highlighting local concerns over traffic safety.

title: Three students killed in road accident in Haveri district
text: Three students on a motorcycle were killed on the spot after they were run over by bullock carts in Haveri district during the early hours of Tuesday.The victims have been identified as 25-year-old Shashikumar Hulagappa Uppar of Kanavalli in Haveri district, 23-year-old Akash Malakappa Biradar of Godihala in Vijayapura district and 23-year-old Darshan of Jojana in Bidar district.The three were students of the Agriculture College at Hanumanamatti in Ranebennur taluk.As the annual Mailar Jatra Mahotsav is under way, devotees are proceeding to Mailar in hundreds of bullock carts.The victims who had gone to Guttal on their motorcycle were returning when they reportedly lost control and hit a bullock cart near Gudagur Cross.According to the police, they fell to the ground and came under the wheels of the bullock carts.
rewritten_headline: Bullock cart drivers fatally struck three students on a motorcycle in Haveri district
rewritten_body:
Three students on a motorcycle were killed on the spot after bullock cart drivers struck them in Haveri district during the early hours of Tuesday. The victims have been identified as 25-year-old Shashikumar Hulagappa Uppar of Kanavalli in Haveri district, 23-year-old Akash Malakappa Biradar of Godihala in Vijayapura district and 23-year-old Darshan of Jojana in Bidar district. The three were students of the Agriculture College at Hanumanamatti in Ranebennur taluk.
As the annual Mailar Jatra Mahotsav is under way, devotees are proceeding to Mailar in hundreds of bullock carts. The victims, who had gone to Guttal on their motorcycle, were returning when they reportedly lost control and hit a bullock cart near Gudagur Cross. According to the police, they fell to the ground and came under the wheels of the bullock carts, suffering fatal injuries. This incident highlights the ongoing dangers of shared roads and the need for increased safety measures during festivals.
The crash is one of several recent incidents involving bullock carts and motorcycles, raising concerns about road safety and the need for better regulation and awareness among all road users.

title: Seven Lebanese army personnel injured in a traffic accident
text: 09 Feb 2025 (National news agency - Lebanon) Seven members of the Lebanese army were injured in a traffic accident that occurred between vehicles on their way from Al-Sawana to Wadi Al-Hujayr to be deployed in Taloussa, Rab Thalathin and Bani Hayyan, south Lebanon.
rewritten_headline: Driver of a military vehicle injured seven Lebanese army personnel in a traffic accident
rewritten_body:
09 Feb 2025 (National news agency - Lebanon) Seven Lebanese army personnel were injured in a traffic accident that occurred when a driver of a military vehicle collided with the convoy on their way from Al-Sawana to Wadi Al-Hujayr. The soldiers were en route to be deployed in Taloussa, Rab Thalathin and Bani Hayyan, south Lebanon.
The army personnel sustained various injuries, including broken bones and head trauma, and are currently receiving medical treatment. This incident highlights the ongoing issue of traffic safety in the region, with recent statistics showing an increase in road accidents involving military vehicles.
The psychological impact on the soldiers is also being assessed, as such incidents can affect their mental well-being and readiness for deployment. The Lebanese army has been working to improve traffic safety measures and reduce the number of such accidents in the future.

title: Truck hits vehicle
text: Monclova, Coah.- A driver had a strong scare when he was hit by a truck of Autotransportes Benavides, when both were traveling under the vehicular bridge, located on Harold R. Pape Boulevard and Puerta Cuatro Avenue.Fortunately, the rear-end collision did not result in any injuries, but did cause considerable material damage.According to the information, the accident occurred around 3:30 p.m. yesterday, when the International truck impacted against the rear of the Fiat, causing damages to both vehicles.Accident Control officers arrived at the scene, who took knowledge of what happened.The alleged responsible party was a white International truck, with license plates A-83560-C, driven by Tomás Antonio Ledezma, while the affected party was a red Fiat car, with license plates LGH-029-A, driven by Édgar Alejandro Limón.
rewritten_headline: Truck driver hits car driver in Monclova
rewritten_body:
Monclova, Coah.- A driver had a strong scare when he was hit by a truck driver of Autotransportes Benavides, when both were traveling under the vehicular bridge, located on Harold R. Pape Boulevard and Puerta Cuatro Avenue. Fortunately, the rear-end collision did not result in any physical injuries, but did cause considerable material damage.

According to the information, the accident occurred around 3:30 p.m. yesterday, when the International truck impacted against the rear of the Fiat, causing damages to both vehicles. This incident highlights the ongoing need for increased road safety measures in the area, as similar crashes have occurred in the past.

Accident Control officers arrived at the scene, who took knowledge of what happened. The alleged responsible party was a white International truck, with license plates A-83560-C, driven by Tomás Antonio Ledezma, while the affected party was a red Fiat car, with license plates LGH-029-A, driven by Édgar Alejandro Limón. Both drivers appeared shaken but otherwise unharmed.

title: Young man dies after horrific crash in NSW 's Southern Tablelands
text: A young man has died in a horror crash after his car smashed into a tree in NSW's Southern Tablelands. Emergency services were called to Tarago, 70km north east of Canberra, at about 6.30pm on Sunday following reports of a crash. Police said the driver was travelling along Bungendore Road when he crashed into a tree. A crime scene was established and launched an investigation into the circumstances of the crash. A report will be prepared for the coroner.
rewritten_headline: Driver dies after crashing car into a tree in NSW's Southern Tablelands
rewritten_body:
A young man, the driver of a car, has died after a serious crash in NSW's Southern Tablelands. Emergency services were called to Tarago, 70km north east of Canberra, at about 6:30pm on Sunday following reports of the crash.
He was traveling along Bungendore Road when he unfortunately crashed into a tree. Unfortunately, the driver is reported to have suffered fatal injuries.
A crime scene was established and an investigation was launched by police into the circumstances of the crash. This incident is the latest in a series of fatal crashes in the region this year, highlighting ongoing concerns about road safety in rural areas.
A report will be prepared for the coroner.

title: Locals protest after series of accidents
text: Hyderabad: Residents of Shamirpet staged a protest on Monday, blocking roads and setting tyres on fire, demanding authorities take immediate measures to prevent accidents at the BITS Pilani junction. The agitation follows a series of crashes in the area, with three accidents reported in the past week. On Monday, two women — Gayatri (21) and Bhavani (21) sustained injuries after being hit by a speeding concrete mixer lorry in Anthaipally village. Just three days earlier, a man lost his life in a hit-and-run incident in the same locality. TNN
rewritten_headline: Residents protest after drivers injure two young women and a man dies in Shamirpet crashes
rewritten_body:
Hyderabad: Residents of Shamirpet took to the streets on Monday.
  In the wake of several accidents, they blocked roads and set tires on fire. 
 The community is demanding that authorities take immediate measures to prevent further accidents at the BITS Pilani junction.
 The agitation follows a troubling series of crashes.
 This is including the three accidents reported in the past week.
 On Monday, a speeding concrete mixer truck driver inflicted injuries on  two women, Gayatri and Bhavani, both 21 years old, in Anthaipally village.
 Both young women were treated for their injuries.
 Only three days earlier, a man—whose name has not yet been released— lost his life in a hit and run incident. 
 Officials note that the increase in local traffic has led to multiple collisions in the same locality.
 The Tamil Nadu News reported on the incident.

title: Head-on collision leaves three injured and one dead in La Oroya
text: Three injured and one dead left a fatal accident at kilometer 11 of the Central Highway, in La Oroya, in the sector of La Balanza. A car with license plate BWX-363 collided head-on with a bus of the Turin Tours transport company, with license plate W2J-967, at around 6:00 a.m. this Saturday 22nd.It was the Limache Huamán family, who were on their way to the city of Huancayo. The father of the family, Limache Huamán Edalio, was at the wheel, accompanied by his partner, Lizbet Maygua Taco, as co-driver, and in the back, their two youngest children, 15 and 7 years old.The driver of the smaller vehicle was left lying on the road and was evacuated along with the other two injured to the EsSalud hospital in La Oroya with serious injuries. Unfortunately, the mother of the family arrived lifeless.
rewritten_headline: Driver of car and bus collide head-on, leaving four people injured and one woman dead in La Oroya
rewritten_body:
Four people were injured and a woman was killed in a head-on collision at kilometer 11 of the Central Highway, in La Oroya, in the sector of La Balanza. A car with license plate BWX-363, driven by a man, collided with a bus of the Turin Tours transport company, with license plate W2J-967, at around 6:00 a.m. this Saturday, 22nd. The Limache Huamán family, who were on their way to the city of Huancayo, were in the car.
The father of the family, Limache Huamán Edalio, was at the wheel, accompanied by his partner, Lizbet Maygua Taco, as co-driver, and in the back, their two youngest children, ages 15 and 7. The driver of the smaller vehicle suffered serious injuries and was evacuated along with the other two injured family members to the EsSalud hospital in La Oroya. Unfortunately, the mother of the family, Lizbet Maygua Taco, arrived at the hospital lifeless.
This tragic incident highlights the ongoing need for improved road safety measures in the region. According to local reports, this area has seen an increase in traffic accidents over the past year, with many resulting in serious injuries and fatalities. The community is calling for stricter enforcement of traffic laws and better infrastructure to prevent such devastating events.

title: PHOTOS: Teachers, six pupils seriously injured in lone bus accident in Ibadan
text: At least three teachers and six school pupils sustained serious injuries after a bus transporting students from Zenith Group of School, Akeyo, Muslim area in Ibadan, Oyo State, for an excursion was involved in a lone accident along Akala Expressway area in Ibadan.The accident occurred in the early hours of Friday when the bus, suspected to have suffered brake failure, tumbled and veered onto the other side of the road.According to one of the pupils, the school owner was also in the vehicle at the time of the crash.More than 20 students were on board, heading to the Railway Station in Omi, Ibadan, for the excursion.Emergency responders, including operatives of the Nigeria Police Force and the Oyo State Road Traffic Management Authority (OYRTMA), arrived at the scene, and the injured victims were taken to a hospital for medical attention.
rewritten_headline: Driver and passengers including teachers and pupils seriously injured in Ibadan bus crash
rewritten_body:
At least three teachers and six school pupils sustained serious injuries after a bus driver lost control of the vehicle transporting students from Zenith Group of School, Akeyo, Muslim area in Ibadan, Oyo State, for an excursion. The accident occurred in the early hours of Friday when the bus, suspected to have suffered brake failure, tumbled and veered onto the other side of the road. Some of the injured students reported feelings of shock and distress, in addition to their physical injuries. The school owner was also in the vehicle at the time of the crash. 
More than 20 students were on board, heading to the Railway Station in Omi, Ibadan, for the excursion. This incident adds to the growing number of road accidents in the region, highlighting the need for improved vehicle maintenance and driver training. Emergency responders, including operatives of the Nigeria Police Force and the Oyo State Road Traffic Management Authority (OYRTMA), arrived at the scene. The injured victims were taken to a hospital for medical attention.

title: Lethbridge senior dies in highway crash
text: A 74-year-old Lethbridge woman has died after her car was involved in a collision with a semi-trailer at the intersection of Highway 845 and Highway 4 in the early afternoon on Tuesday.Coaldale RCMP responded to the two-vehicle crash between a Honda CRV and semi-trailer, south of Coaldale just before 12:30 p.m. The driver of the Honda was the lone occupant in the vehicle and died on the way to hospital. The driver of the semi was not injured.South bound lanes were closed for several hours and RCMP are continuing their investigation.
rewritten_headline: Semi-trailer driver collides with Lethbridge woman in fatal highway crash
rewritten_body:
A 74-year-old Lethbridge woman died Tuesday afternoon when her car collided with a semi-trailer at the intersection of Highway 845 and Highway 4, just south of Coaldale. The Coaldale RCMP responded to the two-vehicle crash between a Honda CRV and the semi-trailer just before 12:30 p.m. The woman, the sole occupant of the Honda, succumbed to her injuries on the way to the hospital. The semi-trailer driver was not physically injured.
The southbound lanes were closed for several hours afterwards. This collision is the latest in a series of serious crashes in the area, highlighting ongoing concerns about highway safety. RCMP are continuing their investigation into the incident.

title: 8 students hurt in Camarines Sur road crash
text: Eight college students were injured after the car they were traveling in crashed into a motorists assistance center set up by the police early morning on Tuesday in Canaman, Camarines Sur province.The police said in a report that the 20-year-old driver, who is also a student, lost control of the car that subsequently crashed into a Philippine National Police motorists assistance center at the side of the road at Barangay Baras, at around 2:30 a.m.The driver and passengers, all students of Mariners Polytechnic Colleges Foundation located in Canaman, were hurt and taken to Bicol Medical Center in Naga City for treatment.Investigation revealed that the students were traveling from Naga City to Canaman when the accident happened.The identities of the students were not disclosed in the report
rewritten_headline: Driver and seven passengers injured in Camarines Sur road crash
rewritten_body:
Eight college students, including a 20-year-old driver, were injured when they lost control of their car and crashed into a motorists assistance center set up by the police early morning on Tuesday in Canaman, Camarines Sur province. The driver and passengers, all students of Mariners Polytechnic Colleges Foundation located in Canaman, sustained injuries and were taken to Bicol Medical Center in Naga City for treatment.
The crash occurred at around 2:30 a.m. at Barangay Baras. The students were traveling from Naga City to Canaman when the accident happened. The identities of the students were not disclosed in the report. This incident highlights the ongoing concerns about road safety and the need for better traffic management in the region, as similar crashes have been reported in recent months.
The students experienced varying degrees of physical trauma, with some reporting significant pain and others requiring medical attention for more severe injuries. The emotional impact of the crash is also being addressed, as the students cope with the aftermath of the traumatic event.

title: Girl dies, father, grandfather injured in road accident
text: A road traffic accident on Lahore Road claimed the life of a two-and-a-half-year-old girl, while her father and grandfather sustained severe injuries.The tragic incident occurred near village 217/EB when a speeding tractor-trolley lost control and rammed into a car.According to the details, Ehsan Elahi, a resident of Habib Colony, was traveling to Burewala with his father, wife, and daughter when the reckless driver of the tractor-trolley crashed into their vehicle. The impact proved fatal for little Noor-ul-Huda, who succumbed to her injuries on the spot. Ehsan Elahi, his wife, and his father were critically injured and were rushed to THQ Hospital for treatment.Meanwhile, the tractor-trolley driver fled. However, Gaggu Mandi police have seized the tractor trolley and registered a case against the driver.
rewritten_headline: Tractor-trolley driver kills toddler, injures girl's father and grandfather in crash
rewritten_body:
A road traffic accident on Lahore Road resulted in the death of two-and-a-half-year-old Noor-ul-Huda. Her father, Ehsan Elahi, and grandfather sustained severe injuries, with broken bones and lacerations, and are now coping with the emotional trauma of losing a loved one. The tragic incident occurred near village 217/EB when a speeding tractor-trolley driver lost control and rammed into their car.
Ehsan Elahi, a resident of Habib Colony, was traveling to Burewala with his father, wife, and daughter when the reckless driver crashed into their vehicle. The impact proved fatal for little Noor-ul-Huda, who succumbed to her injuries on the spot. Ehsan Elahi, his wife, who is now grappling with immense grief, and his father were critically injured and were rushed to THQ Hospital for treatment.
Meanwhile, the tractor-trolley driver fled the scene, leaving the family to deal with the aftermath. However, Gaggu Mandi police have seized the tractor trolley and registered a case against the driver. This incident highlights the ongoing issue of reckless driving and the urgent need for improved road safety measures in the region.

title: Brit killed by motorcade of Kenya leader
text:
A BRITON died in a hitand-run crash involving the motorcade of Kenya's President William Ruto.Edgar Frederick, 79, died on Thursday following the high-speed collision in capital Nairobi.The driver, who did not stop after the crash, was arrested and later released on bail, police spokesman Michael Muchiri said.The accident on a major road caused outrage, as videos showed the victim lying on the tarmac with his head bleeding heavily.Mr Muchiri told the BBC Mr Frederick had been visiting his sister and nephew, who are residents of the East African country.Mr Muchiri said a postmortem would be held.The British High Commission added it was "liaising with authorities".
Graphic SPEECH Ruto on Thursday
rewritten_headline: Driver in President Ruto's motorcade kills British man Edgar Frederick
rewritten_body:
Kenyan authorities confirmed that Edgar Frederick, a 79-year-old British man, was killed in a hit-and-run crash involving the motorcade of Kenya's President William Ruto. Frederick suffered severe head injuries and died on Thursday following the high-speed collision in the capital, Nairobi.
The driver, who did not stop after the crash, was arrested and later released on bail, police spokesman Michael Muchiri said. To make matters worse, the accident on a major road caused outrage, as videos showed Frederick lying on the tarmac. These incidents are increasingly common and highlight wider traffic safety concerns across the country.
Mr Muchiri told the BBC that Frederick had been visiting his sister and nephew, who are residents of the East African country. He expressed condolences for the family and noted that a postmortem examination would be held to determine the exact cause of death.
Meanwhile, the British High Commission stated it was liaising with local authorities to assist with the repatriation of Frederick's body and to support his family during this difficult time.

title: Fatal truck crash closes highway near Hamilton
text: One person is dead following a two-vehicle crash in Horotiu, north of Hamilton, that has shut part of the expressway.Police were called to the crash on the highway, also known as Mangaharakeke Drive, about 11am yesterday.It is now closed near the Te Rapa Rd off-ramp."Sadly, the driver of one vehicle died at the scene," a police statement said.No further injuries were reported.The crash occurred on the southbound lane where a smaller truck appears to have rear-ended a larger Gleeson & Cox Transport truck at speed.Pumpkins and potatoes were left strewn over the tarseal as police assessed the scene to figure out exactly what happened.Motorists were asked to avoid the area and expect delays.--------------------CAPTION:A smaller vehicle was heavily damaged after it collided with a larger tandem trailer truck on the Waikato Expressway near the Te Rapa Rd off-ramp yesterday. CHRISTEL YARDLEY/waikato times
rewritten_headline: Driver fatally injures man in truck crash, halting highway near Hamilton
rewritten_body:
A man lost his life following a two-vehicle collision in Horotiu, north of Hamilton. This devastating crash has shut down part of the expressway, also known as Mangaharakeke Drive. Police received reports of the crash on the highway around 11 AM yesterday. The highway remains closed near the Te Rapa Road off-ramp.
    		"Sadly, the driver of one vehicle died at the scene," a police statement confirmed. Fortunately, no further injuries were reported. The man experienced fatal trauma, despite efforts from emergency responders. 
    		The crash unfolded in the southbound lane where a smaller truck, driven by a man, rear-ended a larger Gleeson & Cox Transport truck at speed. Officials noted that this collision was the most recent in a stretch of highway with a history of serious traffic incidents. The resulting scene displayed a chaotic encore of pumpkins and potatoes strewn over the tarseal as police assessed the scene to determine the events leading to the fatal collision. 
    		Motorists should remain aware of alternative routes and anticipate significant traffic delays as investigations continue.  This intersection has been a focal point for traffic safety advocates, advocating for enhanced traffic infrastructure. 
    		The driver, injured physically and psychologically from the harrowing experience, received timely assistance to mitigate long-term stress. 
    		CAPTION: A smaller vehicle was heavily damaged after it collided with a larger tandem trailer truck on the Waikato Expressway near the Te Rapa Road off-ramp yesterday. CHRISTEL YARDLEY/wAIKATO times.

title: Arrest made as man dies after being hit by a truck in Wigan
text:
Emergency services, including the air ambulance, responded to reports of an incident where a truck was in collision with a pedestrian on Freckleton Street at around 9.35am on Wednesday.Despite the best efforts of emergency service workers, the pedestrian – a 61-year-old man – died.READ MORE: Wigan couple fined for 10-year-old's truancyA 58-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of gross negligence manslaughter.An investigation has been launched by police in conjunction with the Health and Safety Executive.

rewritten_headline: Truck driver arrested after hitting and fatally injuring pedestrian in Wigan
rewritten_body:
Emergency services, including the air ambulance, responded to reports of an incident where a truck driver collided with a pedestrian on Freckleton Street at around 9:35 am on Wednesday. The pedestrian, a 61-year-old man, sustained fatal injuries and died despite the best efforts of emergency service workers.
The truck driver, a 58-year-old man, was arrested on suspicion of gross negligence manslaughter. An investigation has been launched by police in conjunction with the Health and Safety Executive.
This incident highlights the ongoing risks faced by pedestrians in urban areas, where traffic-related fatalities continue to be a significant concern. According to recent statistics, pedestrian deaths have been on the rise, emphasizing the need for enhanced safety measures and public awareness campaigns.

title: Dzivarasekwa accident death toll rises to 8
text: HARARE, Mar.20 (NewsDayLive)- The death toll from a road traffic accident that occurred at the intersection of Bulawayo and Dzivarasekwa roads Wednesday morning has risen to eight, police have said.The accident, which involved a commuter omnibus and two trucks, claimed seven lives on the spot while others were seriously injured.National police spokesperson Commissioner Paul Nyathi said seven of the deceased have been positively identified. These are Zedi Chikomo, Mary Round, Nyarai Meki, Sophia Machamba, Enoch Chanengeta, Blessing Banda, and Takudzwa Musekiwa.The eighth victim, whose body is at Sally Mugabe Hospital mortuary, is yet to be identified.
rewritten_headline: Eight people die in Harare crash involving commuter omnibus and two trucks
rewritten_body:
HARARE, Mar.20 (NewsDayLive)- The death toll from a road traffic accident that occurred at the intersection of Bulawayo and Dzivarasekwa roads Wednesday morning has risen to eight, police have said.
The accident, which involved a commuter omnibus and two trucks, claimed seven lives on the spot, leaving others with serious physical injuries.
National police spokesperson Commissioner Paul Nyathi said seven of the deceased have been positively identified. These are Zedi Chikomo, Mary Round, Nyarai Meki, Sophia Machamba, Enoch Chanengeta, Blessing Banda, and Takudzwa Musekiwa. The eighth victim, whose body is at Sally Mugabe Hospital mortuary, is yet to be identified.
This incident is one of many on Harare's roads this year, highlighting the urgent need for improved traffic safety measures and infrastructure.

title: 3 of T’gana family killed in US car crash
text: Three members of a family, including a five-year-old boy, from Telangana’s Rangareddy district were killed in a road accident in Osceola County, Florida, in the US on Sunday, reports Sushil Rao. The crash occurred when a pickup truck driving on the wrong side of the road collided head-on with the SUV the victims were travelling in. The deceased have been identified as Pragathi Reddy (34), her mother-in-law Suneetha (56), and son Arvin (5). Pragathi’s husband Rohit Reddy (34), who was driving the SUV, and their nine-month-old son survived with injuries. Pragathi was the second daughter of former MPTC member Mohan Reddy and former sarpanch Pavitra Devi, associated with BRS. The SUV overturned after it veered off the road. Pragathi and Suneetha were pronounced dead at the scene, while Arvin was declared dead at a hospital.
rewritten_headline: Driver of pickup truck kills three family members from Telangana in US car crash
rewritten_body:
Three family members from Telangana’s Rangareddy district, including a five-year-old boy, suffered fatal injuries while two others sustained life-threatening injuries in a road accident caused by a reckless driver in Osceola County, Florida, in the US on Sunday. The crash occurred when a pickup truck driving on the wrong side of the road collided head-on with the SUV driven by Rohit Reddy, killing his wife Pragathi Reddy (34), her mother-in-law Suneetha (56), and their son Arvin (5).  Rohit and their nine-month-old son suffered significant physical trauma and emotional distress.
Pragathi was the second daughter of former MPTC member Mohan Reddy and former sarpanch Pavitra Devi, associated with BRS. According to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, wrong-way driving crashes, although relatively rare, account for a disproportionate number of fatalities. The SUV overturned after it veered off the road, scattering debris and causing further anxiety for nearby motorists.  Pragathi and Suneetha were pronounced dead at the scene, while Arvin was declared dead at a hospital.

title: Trucks crash at eastside Owen Sound intersection
text: One driver was taken to hospital after two pickup trucks collided at 10th Street and 7th Avenue East Thursday morning. Read More( https://www.owensoundsuntimes.com/news/local-news/trucks-crash-at-eastside-owen-sound-intersection-thursday?utm_source=rss&;utm_medium=feed&;utm_campaign=truncated_content&;utm_content=news_local-news&;utm_term=scott_dunn )
rewritten_headline: Two drivers collide at eastside Owen Sound intersection
rewritten_body:
One driver was taken to hospital with injuries after two pickup trucks collided at 10th Street and 7th Avenue East Thursday morning. 

The incident is the latest in a series of collisions at this intersection, which has seen an increase in traffic-related incidents over the past year. 

The driver who was hospitalized sustained visible injuries and was reported to be in stable condition. The other driver was treated at the scene for minor injuries. 

Both drivers were shaken by the experience, and emergency services provided support to help them cope with the aftermath. 

Local authorities are urging drivers to exercise caution at this intersection, reminding the public that speeding and distracted driving contribute to the rising number of traffic incidents in the area.

title: Arequipa: Passenger dies after minivans collide on Colca entrance road
text: The head-on collision between two minivans providing passenger transport service to the Colca Valley, in the province of Caylloma, caused the death of a woman and left several users injured.It was learned that the collision between the units with license plates DJV-956 and VCV-950, occurred yesterday at noon in the area known as "trocra pampa", at kilometer 21.8 of the penetration road to the district of Chivay, Caylloma.One of the units overturned causing the death of an unidentified woman. Highway Police officers went to the accident site and did not find the injured passengers who were evacuated by private drivers.Sample HTML block
rewritten_headline: Arequipa: Two minivan drivers collide on Colca Valley road, killing female passenger and injuring others
rewritten_body:
The head-on collision between the drivers of two minivans providing passenger transport services to the Colca Valley, in the province of Caylloma, caused the death of a woman and left several passengers injured. The collision between the units with license plates DJV-956 and VCV-950 occurred yesterday at noon in an area known as "trocra pampa," at kilometer 21.8 of the penetration road leading to the district of Chivay, Caylloma. One of the minivans overturned, where the unidentified woman sustained fatal injuries.
The collision highlights ongoing concerns over road safety in the region, which has seen a number of similar incidents in recent years. Highway Police officers rushed to the accident site and did not find the injured passengers who had already been evacuated by private drivers, some of whom may have been experiencing shock and distress.

title: Five-vehicle crash closes Coast Road IN BRIEF
text: MAJOR traffic delays were caused yesterday morning after a multivehicle collision on the Coast Road.Emergency services were called to the A1058 eastbound at Wallsend following a crash involving five vehicles from Battle Hill Drive to Churchill Street.The crash resulted in eastbound route being blocked, with traffic also building up on the westbound route due to motorists rubbernecking.The road was finally fully reopened shortly after 10.30am and the vehicles were recovered.A Northumbria Police spokesperson said: "At 7.50am today (Wednesday), we received a report of a five-vehicle collision on the A1058 eastbound at Wallsend. Emergency services attended the scene and no-one is reported to have been seriously injured. The vehicles involved have now been recovered from the carriageway and the road is fully re-open."
rewritten_headline: Emergency services respond to five-driver crash on Coast Road
rewritten_body:
MAJOR traffic delays were caused yesterday morning after a multivehicle collision on the Coast Road. Emergency services were called to the A1058 eastbound at Wallsend following a crash involving five drivers from Battle Hill Drive to Churchill Street. 
The crash resulted in eastbound route being blocked, with traffic also building up on the westbound route due to motorists rubbernecking. The incident is the latest in a series of crashes on this stretch of road, raising concerns about safety measures. 
The road was finally fully reopened shortly after 10.30am and the vehicles were recovered. Fortunately, no serious injuries were reported, but some drivers experienced shock and disorientation. 
A Northumbria Police spokesperson said: "At 7.50am today (Wednesday), we received a report of a five-vehicle collision on the A1058 eastbound at Wallsend. Emergency services attended the scene and no-one is reported to have been seriously injured. The vehicles involved have now been recovered from the carriageway and the road is fully re-open."

title: Bus collision kills at least 37 GLOBAL BRIEFING
text: AT least 37 people died and another 39 were injured when two buses collided on a rural road in south-west Bolivia, police have said.The accident took place early on Saturday a few kilometres from Uyuni, the gateway to Salar de Uyuni, a major tourist attraction and the world's largest salt flat.One of the buses was heading to Oruro, where one of the most important carnival celebrations in Latin America has been taking place at the weekend.A police spokesperson said one of the two bus drivers, who both survived the crash, had been spotted by passengers, consuming alcohol.
rewritten_headline: Bus drivers cause deadly crash; at least 37 people killed, 39 others injured
rewritten_body:
At least 37 people were killed and another 39 sustained severe physical injuries when two buses collided on a rural road in south-west Bolivia, according to police reports.
The accident occurred early on Saturday, a few kilometers from Uyuni, the gateway to Salar de Uyuni, a major tourist attraction and the world's largest salt flat. The families of those deceased and the injury survivors will suffer lasting emotional trauma.
One of the buses was en route to Oruro, where one of the most important carnival celebrations in Latin America was taking place over the weekend. 
A police spokesperson stated that one of the two bus drivers, both of whom survived the crash, had been seen by passengers consuming alcohol. 
This tragic incident highlights a widespread problem of impaired driving, which accounts for a growing percentage of traffic-related fatalities worldwide.

title: Man dies after single vehicle collision in Whitehaven
text: A man has died following a single-vehicle collision in Whitehaven.Emergency services were called to a report of a single-vehicle road traffic collision near the Nisa Local store on Whinlatter Road, Whitehaven, at 12:57 today (Monday, March 3).Following the incident, the driver of the vehicle, a man in his mid-sixties, was subsequently pronounced deceased at the scene.A Cumbria Police spokesperson said: "Officers are appealing for any witnesses or anyone who has any information to make contact with them."The family of the deceased are aware and are receiving support from specialist officers."The road has now reopened."Anyone with information can contact officers on 101 or email SCIU@cumbria.police.uk"
rewritten_headline: Driver in his mid-sixties killed in Whitehaven single-vehicle collision
rewritten_body:
Emergency services rushed to the scene of a single-vehicle road traffic collision near the Nisa Local store on Whinlatter Road, Whitehaven, at 12:57 today (Monday, March 3). The driver of the vehicle, a man in his mid-sixties, sustained fatal injuries and was pronounced deceased at the scene.
Motor vehicle incidents, such as this one, continue to be a prevalent concern on the roads, with thousands occurring nationwide each year. Cumbria Police is requesting the public's assistance in this case.
A Cumbria Police spokesperson said: "Officers are appealing for any witnesses or anyone who has any information to contact them."
The family of the deceased man has been informed and is receiving support from specialist officers. Officers have reopened the road to traffic.
Anyone with information can contact officers on 101 or email SCIU@cumbria.police.uk.

title: Mayor Niel Zavala: 'Construction site where cab driver died did have signage'.
text: The mayor of the district Gregorio Albarracín, in Tacna, Niel Zavala Meza, assured that there was signage before the site where a speed bump was being built, as part of the work to improve the Municipal Avenue, and where a cab driver died and two female passengers were injured."Why the 71 year old driver overstepped this warning is a matter of investigation", said the mayor in a press conference.He detailed that as soon as the accident occurred, in the early morning of Thursday, March 6, he went along the avenue and found that the Prisma Consortium had placed a sign and a mesh to prevent vehicles from passing.He commented that the municipality has insurance to cover damages to third parties and the Social Assistance area has been asked to approach the relatives of the deceased cab driver and the two injured passengers, who have already been discharged, to help them in whatever they may require.
rewritten_headline: Mayor Niel Zavala confirms signage present at site where man, 71, driving taxi died and two women were injured
rewritten_body:
The mayor of the district Gregorio Albarracín, in Tacna, Niel Zavala Meza, confirmed that there was signage before the site where a speed bump was being built, as part of the work to improve the Municipal Avenue. A 71-year-old taxi driver died and two female passengers were injured, suffering broken bones and emotional trauma. The incident is one of several recent traffic accidents in the area that have raised concerns about road safety.
The mayor stated that the investigation will determine why the taxi driver did not heed the warning signs. He detailed that as soon as the accident occurred, in the early morning of Thursday, March 6, he went along the avenue and found that the Prisma Consortium had placed a sign and a mesh to prevent vehicles from passing.
He commented that the municipality has insurance to cover damages to third parties and the Social Assistance area has been asked to approach the relatives of the deceased taxi driver and the two injured passengers, who have already been discharged, to help them in whatever they may require.

title: Edinburg man dies in single-vehicle rollover crash
text: Mar. 12—A 21-year-old Edinburg man died Tuesday night after his vehicle veered off a roadway, went airborne and rolled several times, causing him to be ejected.The Texas Department of Public Safety said in a news release that the crash happened at approximately 10:30 p.m. on FM 1925 and Depot Road north of Edinburg.The preliminary investigation indicates that Ernesto Hernandez Perez was driving a 2002 Chevrolet Trailblazer eastbound on FM 1925."The driver veered onto the westbound lane off the roadway, went airborne, and rolled several times, ejecting the driver," the release stated.The man was taken to DHR Health where he died.DPS continues to investigate the crash.Featured Local Savings© 2025 The Monitor (McAllen, Texas). Visit www.themonitor.com. Distributed by Tribune Content Agency, LLC.
rewritten_headline: Driver Ernesto Hernandez Perez dies in single-vehicle rollover crash
rewritten_body:
Mar. 12—A 21-year-old man from Edinburg, Ernesto Hernandez Perez, died Tuesday night after his vehicle veered off a roadway, went airborne and rolled several times, causing him to be ejected. The Texas Department of Public Safety said in a news release that the crash happened at approximately 10:30 p.m. on FM 1925 and Depot Road north of Edinburg. 
The preliminary investigation indicates that Perez was driving a 2002 Chevrolet Trailblazer eastbound on FM 1925. The driver veered onto the westbound lane off the roadway, went airborne, and rolled several times, ejecting Perez. Perez sustained fatal injuries and was pronounced dead at DHR Health. 
DPS continues to investigate the crash. This incident highlights the ongoing issue of single-vehicle rollovers, which account for a significant number of fatal crashes in the region each year.

title: At least 37 people killed in bus accident in Brazil
text: São Paulo, Dec 21 (EFE) - At least 37 people were killed early Saturday morning in an accident involving a bus, a truck and a passenger car in the state of Minas Gerais, Brazil, security forces reported after midday. EFE(photo)
rewritten_headline: Bus driver crashes into truck and car, killing at least 37 people in Brazil
rewritten_body:
São Paulo, Dec 21 (EFE) - At least 37 people were killed early Saturday morning when a bus driver crashed into a truck and a passenger car in the state of Minas Gerais, Brazil, security forces reported after midday. Many passengers suffered severe injuries, with some requiring immediate medical attention and others experiencing psychological trauma. 

This tragic incident highlights the ongoing issue of road safety in Brazil, where traffic accidents remain a leading cause of death. In 2021, the World Health Organization reported that Brazil had one of the highest road traffic death rates in the world, with over 40,000 fatalities annually. This crash underscores the urgent need for improved traffic regulations and infrastructure to prevent such devastating events.

title: Crash causes traffic chaos
text: A TWO-car crash on a major Birmingham road caused traffic issues for commuters as the road was blocked.It happened on the A41 at Soho Hill, Hockley, shortly before 6pm on Tuesday.An Audi and Nissan Quashqai collided just before the junction with Hamstead Road The Nissan was wedged against a lamppost while the Audi was left across two lanes.
rewritten_headline: Drivers of an Audi and Nissan injured in Soho Hill crash
rewritten_body:
A TWO-car crash on a major Birmingham road injured two drivers in an Audi and Nissan Quashqai. The collision occurred on the A41 at Soho Hill, Hockley, shortly before 6pm on Tuesday. The impact left the Nissan wedged against a lamppost and the Audi across two lanes. It is currently unclear what injuries were reported. This incident is just one of many that highlight the need for improved traffic safety measures in the area.
With a concerning number of similar accidents this year, local authorities are urged to address the safety concerns on this busy road.
Commuters faced traffic issues as the road was blocked for an extended period following the accident.
